programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part special, a lone detective is on the trail of a Luton man who sexually abused scores of young people, in what becomes a very complex and disturbing pursuit.

programme.title: Manhunt
programme.desc: A known criminal reportedly carrying a gun is heading for a Manchester music concert. Specialist police units race to find and neutralise the threat before he enters the venue.

programme.title: Sky Coppers
programme.desc: The Drone Unit search for a man fleeing the scene of a dramatic hit and run. Rival footie fans cause panic on the streets of Wolverhampton. A call comes in about a body buried in a park.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Car S.O.S
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw seek out classic cars with deserving owners. Using all their guile and restoration knowledge they sneak away clapped out classics without their owners knowing then and get the much loved motors back on the road.

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: An eccentric man commands Mike and Frank to buy; rare cars; a freestyle shop in Kentucky.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry and Simon get revved up in Pembrokeshire as they explore mechanic Jamess sheds, which are crammed with classic cars, motorbikes and tractors, including a 1928 Model A Ford.

programme.title: Chateau DIY: Win the Dream
programme.desc: Four new pairs arrive, desperate to stand out during their first team challenge: transforming tired outbuildings into luxurious dining rooms.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Karen and Richard love Cyprus so much they got married there. Now, theyre after their own slice of the Med in Famagusta. Can Jean Johansson find them the perfect holiday home for £200k?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Brian and Sue want a place all of their family can enjoy in the relaxed beachside town of La Cala de Mijas in Spain. Jean Johansson shows them what £200k can buy.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host from Ipswich is bubbly theatre lover Claire Flatt, who is hoping to impress with her panto-themed dinner party. Claire serves up Thai fishcakes and Princess Jasmine rice as well as her signature `chaos cake for four strangers.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Basingstoke. First host, marketing manager and maths enthusiast Nicole, hopes to wow her guests with a culinary journey, from Lebanon to the Caribbean, and a disco for good measure.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phils got a pair of party people looking for a pad with all the cocktail bars Balham has to offer on their doorstep. Meanwhile Kirsties helping a classical musician and lawyer keen to enjoy the rural feel of Wimbledon.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Phil returns to Stockport to uncover an unexpected twist in the story of Renee and Jacqueline, who were at love/list loggerheads in 2023.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In south London, Kirstie and Phil help Jess and Zac, whove viewed over 60 properties to no avail, and then help self-professed oldest first-time buyers in town, Rachel and Alan.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The award-winning show returns - this time in Nottingham and Queens Medical Centre, one of the busiest AandEs in Europe, where young Hunters been rushed after falling from a tree.

programme.title: Secret Genius
programme.desc: In the final heat, twelve more contestants take on a dizzying anagram, a twist on Guess Who, and a bamboozling clock conundrum. But only three can go through to the finals.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The Goggleboxers gets stuck into a new series and more of the biggest and best TV. What do they think this time?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: The Great Icelandic Swim with Ross Edgley
programme.desc: Sixty-foot waves, nosy killer whales, hypothermia, tongue rot. Ultra-athlete Ross Edgley attempts to swim all the way around Iceland - 1000 miles of icy water.

programme.title: Lake District Rescue
programme.desc: The Lake District Mountain Rescue Association help the lost, injured and stricken. A man with a dislocated shoulder is stranded at the popular Eskdale Pools. Miles from the nearest road, the challenge is how to transport the casualty to a hospital.

programme.title: Lake District Rescue

programme.title: Extreme Tow Truckers
programme.desc: The Parks Towing team battle a paint-filled truck blocking Highway 1; Claytons Towing go head-to-head with the tide to salvage a sinking houseboat.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: A man weaves a stolen motorbike through traffic at high speeds as he tries to evade police; deputies examine the aftermath of a violent domestic argument; a mum and dad deny that their habits could affect their child.

programme.title: Winter Road Rescue
programme.desc: Snowplough drivers and patrols are prepared to work all day and all night to keep UK roadways clear from obstruction when winter sweeps the nation. Fearless in the face of a cold front, these men and women are there to assist when disaster strikes.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Katie Eustace is in the middle of chasing some criminals that seem to have no limits.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: S18/Eptors S18 E13 - Reality series following high-speed police intercept units in Nottingham.

programme.title: Vera Cruz
programme.desc: Two trigger-happy cowboy mercenaries head for Mexico during the 1866 revolution. A pair of beautiful women try to persuade them to fight for rival armies, but they eventually side with the Emperor Maximilian, tempted by the consignment of gold that they have been hired to escort. Inevitably, revolutionary fervour and double-dealing point the way to a violent confrontation.

programme.title: Trucking Heavy
programme.desc: In Essex, hardcore heavy haulier Ian is assigned to shift a 58-tonne metal compactor, after dark and in the rain.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: In North Yorkshire, the traffic cops aim to protect lorry drivers and their cargo from organised gangs that target goods and trucks. In one case, a hijack is attempted on an HGV carrying £1 million in goods.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jenno joins a TPAC-takedown of a man suspected to be armed, Dan and Gordy hit the door of a suspected drug den, Ian nicks a driver who is so drunk that he cannot even put his shoes on the right feet, and Steve rushes to help a pregnant lady caught up in a three-car smash.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders
programme.desc: On the nightshift in Newcastle, PCs Jan Simlesa and Jamie Leslie spot a driver with his foot down in a top of the range BMW. Meanwhile, a force-wide hunt for two stolen cars leads Jan, Marc and Jamie to the seaside town of Whitley Bay.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jim and the team take to the road to spring a trap on suspected drug dealers, while Dan and Gordy track down a man seen roaming the streets armed with two knives. John picks up the pieces of an unbelievable multi-car pile-up, plus an early morning raid sees the Interceptors bust through doors in search of class-A drugs.

programme.title: Police: Hour Of Duty
programme.desc: Fighting crime against the clock: the officers of Derbyshire Police Force tackle a range of incidents, from drug house stakeouts to missing children and domestic violence calls, all in one hectic real-life hour.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: Roads policing officer PC Merchant is in pursuit of a stolen car who doesnt know the country roads and is driving dangerously. Two cars have crashed at a junction, there are reports of both parties injured and one of the cars is smoking.

programme.title: Banged up Abroad
programme.desc: When police raid his cocaine lab, drug dealer Pieter Tritton is forced to go on the run but he cant resist collecting one final shipment from Ecuador. He soon finds himself in one of the worlds most violent and corrupt penal systems.

programme.title: Extreme Tow Truckers
programme.desc: Claytons Towing tackles a devastating 80-tonne crane collapse, Wagga Towing recovers a rolled B-double, and Parks Towing attend to a stolen car recovery in boulder-ridden Port Hills.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.sub-title: Journey to the End of the Earth
programme.desc: Polar drivers start up the longest road in the world, VP stays closer to home.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Alpha Recovery join the Trucking Hell team as Kev is tasked with rescuing a 45-tonne mobile crane with a blown engine. Not only is the crane stranded on the A1 at the height of rush hour, its also marooned in the midst of major road works.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors chase a runaway van, the unit helps a man involved in a serious farming accident, and the air support crew hunt down a car thief.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: At Barnsley Casualty the team clock on to a backlog of patients waiting to be transferred to wards.With no beds across the entire hospital, Sister Jane Hawksworth is battling to prevent patients beingon trollies for the NHS maximum of 12 hours.

programme.desc: The interceptors pursue an out-of-control motorbike, a petrol thief attempts to destroy his ill-gotten gains, and one member of the team gets the chance to ride in a brand-new police vehicle.

programme.desc: The casualty department treats a broken leg from a forklift, a debilitating neurological disease, a possible spinal infection, and an injury from a fall.

programme.sub-title: Togail: A-Listers in Lewis and Harris
programme.desc: Jayne MacLeod goes on a fascinating tour of the eight A-Listed buildings in Lewis and Harris. She begins her journey in Ness at the Butt of Lewis Lighthouse where she speaks to the retained lighthouse keeper, who tells her about the history of this Stevenson building and how the job has changed over the years.

programme.title: Transatlantic Sessions
programme.desc: Fans of the Sessions can see performances from James Taylor, Kathy Mattea, Dougie MacLean, Ricky Skaggs as well as the late Nanci Griffith.

programme.title: Plaigh air Bord
programme.desc: In 1952, British scientists moored a floating laboratory off Tolsta on the Isle of Lewis to animal test the efficiency of viruses, including bubonic plague, as biological weapons. Bubonic plague once killed half the population of Europe, so the government was appalled when a fishing boat with a crew of 18 sailed through a cloud of virus. The experiments were hushed up, and the fishermen never told of their exposure to the plague.

programme.title: Dàna: Scotlands Wild Side
programme.desc: Coinneach is in his element this week as he traverses the incredible skyline of the Rum Cullin, high above the clouds.

programme.title: Seo BBC Alba
programme.desc: A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: Seo BBC Alba
programme.desc: A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: British Historys Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy debunks the foundation myth of one of our favourite royal dynasties, the Tudors. According to the history books, after 30 years of bloody battles between the white-rosed Yorkists and the red-rosed Lancastrians, Henry Tudor rid us of civil war and the evil Richard III. But Lucy reveals how the Tudors invented the story of the Wars of the Roses after they came to power to justify their rule.

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Chinas heartland is the centre of a 5,000-year-old civilisation and is home to the giant panda, the golden snub-nosed monkey and the golden takin. China faces environmental problems, but the relationship the Chinese have with their environment is deep and extraordinary. We will understand what this means for the future of China.

programme.title: Call My Bluff
programme.desc: Panel game of word definitions and deceptions, hosted by Robert Robinson. Team captains Frank Muir and Patrick Campbell are joined by Patrick Mower, Russell Harty, Nanette Newman and Gabrielle Drake.

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Polly Elwes, Richard Baker and John Julius Norwich. With guest musician Yvonne Minton.

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: The End of the Solar System
programme.desc: This is the story of how our solar system will be transformed by the ageing Sun, before coming to a spectacular end in about eight billion years. Astronomers can peer into the far future to predict how it will happen by analysing distant galaxies, stars and even planets in their final moments.

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Twelve billion miles away a spaceship is leaving our solar system and entering the void of deep space. Slowly dying within its heart is a plutonium generator that will beat for perhaps another decade before the lights on Voyager finally go out.

programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke reveals how sculpture communicates our most cherished beliefs and values, the British soul captured in three dimensions. To begin, he looks at medieval sculpture, from Gothic cathedrals to the misericords underneath church stalls. He shows how sculpture casts a new light on medieval Britain, a more sophisticated, fun-loving and maverick place than commonly believed.

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Polly Elwes, Richard Baker and John Julius Norwich. With guest musician Yvonne Minton.

programme.title: The Australian Wars
programme.desc: The story of Australias first wars, calling for the nation to acknowledge the First Peoples who died in these conflicts and for the Australian War Memorial to recognise them.

programme.title: City Scapes

programme.title: Wogan
programme.sub-title: Kenneth Williams, Barbara Windsor, Stephen Fry, Michael Palin
programme.desc: Kenneth Williams stands in for Terry Wogan, chatting with guests Barbara Windsor and husband Steve Hollings, Stephen Fry and Michael Palin. Music by Hank Marvin and The Shadows.

programme.title: The Kenneth Williams Story: A Reputations Special
programme.desc: A look at one of Britains funniest and most complex comic performer. From a childhood dominated by his adoring mother and bullying father to a lonely suicidal end tortured with regrets, it spans an extraordinary life and career using clips from his unforgettable performances on radio, stage, film and TV; interviews with those who knew him best.

programme.title: Comic Roots
programme.desc: Kenneth Williams explores his roots in and around Londons St Pancras, where he grew up. Architecture, poetry, art and music were the formative influences on Kenneth Williams: the slum architecture of St Pancras where he grew up; the liquid poetry of his grans fruity anecdotes; the art of the Marcel wave practised by his hairdresser father; and the musical knees-up of `The Boot.

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: A Storyville documentary that follows a global team of undercover officers as they penetrate the most hidden corners of the internet to stop those who exploit and harm children.

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: When Russia invades Ukraine, a quiet schoolteacher in a small industrial town picks up his camera and becomes an unlikely dissident. Pavel Talankin isnt a journalist or activist. He organises school events, makes quirky videos and is loved by his students. But when patriotic lessons and military drills begin replacing regular classes, Pavel starts filming. What emerges is a chilling portrait of how propaganda seeps into everyday life and how even children are drawn into the machinery of war.

programme.title: The Kenneth Williams Story: A Reputations Special
programme.desc: A look at one of Britains funniest and most complex comic performer. programme.desc: Banjo has been commissioned by fish shop manager and friend Sally, who wants to modernise her familys traditional business in Tobermory. With his band of trusty locals, including Eoghann the carpenter and handyman Tom, Banjo takes huge risks in realising his design ambitions.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides

programme.title: The Weakest Link

programme.title: Alan Carrs Picture Slam
programme.desc: Playing this week are married couple Stewart and Ross from Glasgow, couple Amy and Anwar from London, and uncle-niece duo George and Danielle from Liverpool. Theyll be taking on four fun and fast-paced rounds - including the brand new Picture Twist and buzzer rounds - that could throw up anything from historical figures to album covers. Its an addictive quiz for the whole family - youll find it hard not to play along from home as well.

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: The fierce Drag Race UK spin-off continues as our international queens fight to impress Mama Ru and do their countries proud. Phasers set to stun! The queens become Space Girls as they tackle the iconic girl group challenge - but with an inter-gay-lactic twist. Drag Race superfan and chart topper RagnBone Man drops in to give the queens a masterclass in vocals, while girl group legends Bananarama join RuPaul, Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel.

programme.desc: Ninas work husband, Clay, puts a strain on things between her and Lee. But an invitation from Lee to an open mic night gives Nina a chance to find out where she stands.

programme.desc: Ghosts Adam and Barbara try to scare off the new owners of their home with their new-found powers, but get more than they bargained for when the new family appear to enjoy being haunted. Desperate, the pair enlist the help of a demonic phantom, despite having been strongly advised against it.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: After a three-minute video featuring a female prison officer and an inmate having sex inside HMP Wandsworth goes viral, award-winning journalist Mobeen Azhar begins an investigation into what happened and why. A year of twists, turns and damning revelations pulls back the curtain on a dangerous wider world of coercion, corruption and contraband thriving behind bars.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: As Mobeens investigation deepens, it becomes clearer that illicit relationships between prisoners and jail staff are just the tip of the iceberg. The bigger story involves organised criminal gangs determined to cash in on a lucrative prison drug economy. When news breaks of a prison governor being jailed for a relationship with a drug-dealing gang boss, Mobeen becomes determined to uncover the true extent of criminal influence inside our prisons.

programme.desc: Dylan the cargo train driver is determined to deliver all his cargo and will not be derailed, no matter what obstacles get in the way.

programme.title: Vegesaurs

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey, Bingo and Missy take to the high seas and play pirates on the hammock swing, but theyll need to gather all their courage to take on storms and whale attacks.

programme.desc: A severe weather warning from Duggees barometer leads the Squirrels to round up all the creatures they can find outside and pile into the clubhouse for an impromptu party, earning their Get Indoors badges in the process!

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers land on Planet Labyrinth, which looks like a world full of colourful plants with all kinds of shapes. The inhabitants are small fuchsia, flying creatures that surround the bushes and change them constantly.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Timmy and his friends are playing ball games, catch, crazy golf, tennis and so on. Stripey just cant get the hang of hand-to-eye co-ordination and is upset when Yabba gets impatient with him.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Amanpreet challenges her sister Amrit to a biking competition to see who can cycle around the bike track the fastest. The bikes get really muddy, so Amanpreet takes charge of giving them a good clean and has some splashy fun.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Kugelis is a recipe from Lithuania that contains bacon and grated potato. Harry has so many potatoes to grate that he has to get his sister to help him! They both work incredibly hard to prepare the dish, which also uses eggs and spring onions. Harry cleverly decorates his table using traditional, beautifully coloured Lithuanian sashes. The kugelis is served with sour cream - lets hope Harrys friends love the taste!

programme.title: Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Elena finds out all about marvellous moths. Chantelle is with Dr Amir Khan to see one of the worlds biggest moths while Rory and his son Sandy are in disguise being moth spies.

programme.desc: Dan the Border collie is a search and rescue dog who works with his owner Jamie. His super nose follows scents and smells like clues so they can rescue people who need their help. Dan and Jamie have been called to the care home by police officer Penny because an elderly gentleman has gone missing. His name is Robert, but he likes to be called Pop-Pop. He only moved into the care home recently and loves to explore, but he sometimes loses his way Dan is a mountain dog who loves an urban rescue.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Sunbird and Day Gecko
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie try to meet sunbirds, but they are hard to spot. Fast-moving and tiny, they fly quickly between the branches. When the pair finally catch sight of them, they manage to see these beautifully coloured birds up close.

Angela tries to settle Waffle but he whines, hes missing his Brooklyn-Bell family and looks at their photo on the wall of his kennel.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say u, and we go on an adventure around the sound. We go up, then underground, and meet an upbeat umbrella! programme.desc: N likes to say no - but he meets his match when the dog, the cat and the pig wont help him make his bread. This episode has fun with the words dog, dig, cat, cut, pig, pick, mill, mix, hot and ten.

programme.desc: Grace shows off three amazing machines that are all very little. Grace uses a little pick up truck to take some hay to the horses, shows off a little plane that can only take one person and then goes for a ride on a mini motorbike.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea steals Tomatos precious jewel. Supertato ventures to get it back by secretly lifting the jewel from Evil Peas crown and defeating a crowd of rampaging Mini Peas.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: The towns ferris wheel is stuck, so Mojo and Bo have to get it moving. But they do their job too well, because the wheel rolls away, forcing Mojo and Bo to chase it down.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey is unable to get to sleep, so Mum reads her one last story. Unicorse, the most annoying hand puppet in the world, interrupts them, and Bluey tries to reform it.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Bingo drops Mums breakfast-in-bed on the floor, shes convinced shes ruined her big day. Bluey finds a way to cheer her sister up and they put on a show about how Mum and Dad first met.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Pancake is persuaded by Nugget to have a massive birthday party, but when the bash grows increasingly over the top, he finds his own unique way to handle the pressure.

programme.title: Andys Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Jen is trying to put together an audio database of animal sounds for Mr Hammonds birthday, but one sound, resembling a howl, eludes them. Andy jets off in his safari-mobile to the Sonoran desert in America in search of the mystery sound and the animal that made it. Along the way, he meets a grasshopper mouse and escapes a pack of hungry harris hawks.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle learns that the summer holidays will soon be over and Evie will be going to school. Determined not to be left alone, Waffle hatches a plan to keep the family at home.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Its Rafis seventh birthday, and in her family of Wishing Wizards, that means shes finally old enough to receive her very own wishing wand. With her dads, Dash and Jake, Rafi learns the three important rules of wishing: you can only make one wish a day, wishes always run out and you cant unwish a wish. As Rafi holds her wand for the first time, she realises the possibilities are endless - and a little bit magical. Rafis birthday marks the beginning of a whole new chapter.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi loves bouncing on the trampoline and wishes she could keep bouncing all day. Her feet become bouncy like trampolines, and at first it feels brilliant. But bouncing everywhere makes simple jobs tricky - cups topple over, eggs splatting, and even hugs are hard. Rafi doesnt want help, but soon she sees that bouncing all the time isnt as fun as she thought. With her dads, she realises its okay to ask for help. Together they share pancakes, laughter, and a big upside-down hug.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: In the Eye of the Storm
programme.desc: In 2024, hundreds of cameras capture hurricanes slamming Floridas Gulf Coast. As category four Hurricane Helene brings record flooding, residents have no chance to recover before category three Hurricane Milton unleashes over 40 tornadoes.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown
programme.desc: With unprecedented access, Josh digs in front of Petras Treasury. Searching for clues to the mysterious civilisation that created one of the new Seven Wonders of the World, Josh rappels down a seven-story cliff face to perform a high-tech scan.

programme.desc: Tony Robinson delves into a pivotal day in the history of the modern world, examining the key events that made VE Day such a momentous 24 hours. programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: Douglas finds marriage agrees with him - for all of two weeks. When it all blows up his entire fortune is at stake, and its up to Jen, Roy, Moss and one other mysterious Reynholm Industries employee to help him out.

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: A one-off special episode written and directed by Graham Linehan. The reputations of Roy, Jen and that of Reynholm Industries are at risk when an incident involving spilt coffee and a homeless person ends up on the internet.

programme.title: Green Wing
programme.desc: Guy has to live without a driving licence and Statham has to live without sex - will either of them survive? Meanwhile, just when Caroline thought shed climbed all the ladders, she slips down the longest snake on the board, and ends up back at square one.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Miles Jupp go against Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe. Sam Simmons joins Countdowns resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: New. At the halfway stage, all contestants - except Phil Wang - are in with a chance of victory. Kerry Godliman and Jessica Knappett resort to soapy violence.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Nicolas been divorced twice but is still young at heart and hopes for a new love. Mark, who has Crohns disease, has lost his body confidence but is now ready to conquer his fear.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Dean plans a series of controlled disasters for the `Wedding that Goes Wrong. Will bride Kayleigh see the funny side of setting someone on fire at the ceremony?

programme.title: Hollyoaks: Omnibus
programme.desc: Froggy shares memories with Rex as they scatter Graces ashes. Ro makes a decision about his future. The McQueens panic when the police turn up. A couple are forced to face their feelings.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: The McQueens panic when the police turn up on their doorstep. The wheels are in motion for Donnys new business. And a confession breaks a residents heart.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Protestors fill the police station after a villager is wrongfully imprisoned. Will the truth come out? One resident jumps to the wrong conclusions, and a kind gesture has ulterior motives.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tubes naughty little sister, featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorcycle and bicycle variety.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon fills viewers in on the most contentious restaurant he has ever tried to rescue: Amys Baking Company in Scottsdale, Arizona. Find out what happened to husband-and-wife team Amy and Samy.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Pantaleones in Denver, Colorado, where he meets the Italian restaurants owner Pete, who is truly stuck in the past. Pete stubbornly maintains that he makes the `Best Pizza in Denver, based on a review from the 1980s.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray is honoured for his business skills. Barrys scheme to steal golf carts goes off the rails. Beverly attempts to help Murray find a hobby.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.sub-title: The Day After the Day After
programme.desc: A TV movie inspires Barry to try to convince Murray to build an underground bunker. Meanwhile, Erica and Adam go all out with Mothers Day gifts before making requests of Beverly.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay and Glorias Valentines Day causes a stir when Phil convinces Jay to spice things up with role play. Mitch and Cam ruin Lukes date.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The family stay in Hayleys bosss country house, where there is just one, rather bizarre rule. Gloria and Mitch get invites to a bash at Oprahs.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Arvada, Colorado, where husband-and-wife owners Randy and Alexa have allowed their Old Neighborhood restaurant to fall into utter disrepair.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Queens, New York, where he finds a family torn apart by the stress of running failing Greek restaurant Kati Allo. Husband and wife owners Manny and Christine are on the brink of divorce, and daughter Evelyn is caught in the middle.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and Charles take Terry on a camping trip to help him relieve stress. Amy enlists the help of Gina in trying to convince the NYPD to purchase a product she invented as a child. Meanwhile, Holt tries to help Rosa break up with Marcus.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and Amys relationship gets in the way as they work a case involving a new street drug. Charles panics after damaging one of Holts prized possessions, and Terry teaches Rosa about discipline.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Milhouses parents split up: his mum gets together with an American Gladiator called Pyro, his dad self-produces an album, and Milhouse goes a bit crazy. Worried that Marge might divorce him too, Homer decides to divorce her first and re-marry her.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa develops a crush on Nelson the bully, but his friends resent him having a girlfriend. Can she get him to change his ways?

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray takes Adam out for a driving lesson and they learn something new about Pops in the process.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam and Barry write a song but dont get the reaction they were expecting from Murray. Beverly repeatedly calls Erica at college.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil stumbles into an exciting new career after dropping by Lukes college, while Claire deals with Jay and the merger of Pritchetts Closets with a tech-savvy, hipper company.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Haley cant seem to choose between her past and present relationships and is forced to make a decision.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Jake insults Hitchcock and Scullys work ethic in the precinct, they force their way into one of his cases in attempt to prove themselves.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake isnt sure what to think when an unstable detective returns to the Nine-Nine after being undercover for years. Meanwhile, the precincts custodian takes issue with Charles rebellious behaviour and Holt asks Gina to direct a video.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Sideshow Bob is once again released from prison, into the care of his respectable brother Cecil. Bob takes a job on Cecils dam project, but Bart and Lisa suspect hes up to no good.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart tries everything he can to destroy Lisas image as a dependable babysitter when she is left in charge.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: A discovery sparks a police search at the school. Dodger sets out to prove that Smithy is corrupt. Vickys confidence is crushed. And tensions erupt over two clashing engagement parties.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge make a major mistake when they loan Lisa to Broadway actress Laney Fontaine.

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off: Stand Up To Cancer
programme.desc: For their Signature challenge, Jodie Whittaker, Paloma Faith, Munya Chawawa and Spencer Matthews make cake pops, before taking on a twisted New York favourite in the Technical. And in the Showstopper, they must recreate Paul and Prue in biscuit form.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics return for a new series to share more of their insightful and passionate takes on the weeks biggest and best shows.

programme.title: Dating Naked UK
programme.desc: After the first dumping, the daters are still seeking their connections. With some relationships severed, the way is paved for new ones to grow. A game of Dare or Bare leaves one dater with a regrets.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: YouTuber Jay has no problem sliding into girls DMs but cant make the connections in real life. Victoria from Liverpool has a thing for younger women and is after her very own Xena Warrior Princess.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: With nine million Brits now looking for love online, this series offers the opportunity to experience what its like to meet potential partners from the internet for the first time. In this first episode, Faliraki DJ Terry wants to tame his wild love life and find a girl to settle down with, but the temptation of a gorgeous girl proves difficult to resist on his date night.

programme.title: Lucky Break
programme.desc: James is a inept robber determined to escape under the cover of mounting governor Grahan Mortimers self-penned musical based on the life of Nelson. Even within the prison walls, theres a chance for romance with visiting psychiatrist Annabel Sweep but will he, together with Roger Chamberlain, Rudy and Cliff, be able to join her on the outside?

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.desc: Lucindha Lawson talks about how football and futsal have been constants amidst the struggles in her life and how, despite the many obstacles life has thrown at her, she has achieved her dreams.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Broken Lance
programme.desc: Tensions erupt within an Arizona cattle barons household when his three sons vie for control of the ranch.

programme.title: The Black Swan
programme.desc: With the pardon of notorious pirate Henry Morgan by the English King, Caribbean pirates are offered amnesty if they give up their lawless ways. As governor of Jamaica, Morgan picks Captain Jamie Waring to round up the renegade captain Billy Leech. Beautiful Margaret Denby complicates matters for Waring, as her scorn for him is matched only by his love for her.

programme.title: Criss Cross
programme.desc: An armoured car guard must join a robbery after being caught with his ex wife by her gangster husband.

programme.title: Warlock
programme.desc: After ridding a town of outlaws, gunslinger Curt is challenged by one of the men who helped him. Once the mission is completed, he changes his unruly ways to become town sheriff.

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
programme.desc: The British Empires greatest adventurer, Allan Quatermain, is enlisted to battle masked criminal Fantom. Quatermain needs help, and calls up a band of celebrated heroes: vampiress Mina, Dr Jekyll and the Invisible Man.

programme.title: M3GAN
programme.desc: M3GAN is a marvel of artificial intelligence, a lifelike doll thats programmed to be a childs best friend. When a brilliant roboticist gives her young niece a M3GAN prototype, it soon becomes violent and does anything to protect the girl.

programme.title: Late Night With the Devil
programme.desc: In 1977 a live television broadcast goes horribly wrong, unleashing evil into the nations living rooms.

programme.title: Radical
programme.desc: Sixth grade students at Jose Urbina Lopez Elementary are among the worst performing in Mexico, with their world one of violence and hardship, not possibility. Its the perfect place for new teacher Sergio Juarez to try something different.

programme.title: Chef Grudge Match
programme.desc: Host Laila Ali challenges chefs to settle their disputes in a single round, head-to-head culinary battle. The winners walk out with $10,000 cash, a prized knife from the losing chefs knife roll and bragging rights for life!

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Ride Route 66
programme.desc: In small town America, the bikers enjoy some retro roadside spectacles that provide entertainment and intrigue, including the huge fibreglass figures known as `muffler men. Dave and Si then enjoy a championship winning apple pie and learn how Route 66 entered American folklore by connecting small town America from east to west and enabling it to prosper.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.sub-title: Home Sweet Home: Hotter and Hotter
programme.desc: Ree Drummond turns up the heat in the ranch kitchen with some seriously spicy eats. She transforms Jalapeno Pimento Cheese Dip into a Fiery Grilled Cheese, gives Roasted Chipotle Fish Tacos a real kick and makes hot and cheesy Baked Meatballs.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.sub-title: Home Sweet Home: Mom Knows Best
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is making her visiting moms favourite food, starting with Chocolate Mousse and White Clam Pizza. Because her mum is interested in eating healthy, Ree also makes her So-Good-For-You Salad and Garlic Spinach Spaghetti.

programme.title: Jamies 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Glazed Pork Fillet and Tapas Bruschetta
programme.desc: Jamie makes glazed pork fillet, cajun-style pepper rice and barbecue sauce, and tapas bruschetta, and golden grilled sardines.

programme.title: Jamies 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Lamb Kofte and Keralan Veggie Curry
programme.desc: On the menu this time is lamb kofte, pitta and greek salad, and Keralan veggie curry, poppadoms, rice and minty yoghurt.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Scrumptious cooking series with Ina Garten. Ina cooks with a classic ingredient, chicken. Her chicken salad and rotisserie chicken satay are real corkers.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: The delightful Ina Garten reveals her cooking tips. Ina cooks farm fresh food including scalloped tomatoes and crisp salad, and she hosts her very own question and answer session.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: `The Great British Bake Off makes its debut on Channel 4 as the series returns for an eighth season with 12 budding bakers competing to be crowned the champion. Established judge Paul Hollywood is joined by food connoisseur Prue Leith, while seasoned entertainers Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding take on the role of being the shows hosts. Despite the change in personnel, the key Bake Off traditions remain intact, as the contestants try to impress the panel with a fine selection of beautifully-crafted and delicious cakes, tarts, breads and more.

programme.title: James Martin Digs Deep

programme.title: James Martin Digs Deep

programme.desc: James cooks two of his grannys recipes with his homegrown gooseberries and carrots. He then takes off on his motorbike for a charity bike run delivering chocolate to a childrens hospital.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day, the one course every chef wants to cook at the banquet. Judging the chefs dishes are Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton and guest judge John Williams MBE, executive chef of The Ritz.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the final day of the competition, and the last chance for the eight champions to get one of their dishes to the banquet for Queen Elizabeth lls Great Britons. Judges Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton will judge the dessert course and then decide on the banquet menu to be served at the Houses of Parliament. Joining them in the judging chamber is Grace Dent, restaurant critic, journalist and author.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs arrive at the Palace of Westminster to cook for the Banquet of Great Britons. The chefs have been selected because their dishes showcase contemporary British cuisine and demonstrate the transformation in British food during the Queens historic reign.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs meet their surprise veteran judge and cook their summery starters. A dish celebrating childhood summer memories takes on a complicated courgette dish and a technically tricky take on the cucumber sandwich.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more intense as all the chefs take risks with their dishes. Mike Reid, Executive Chef at M Restaurants in London, shocks with when he pairs his fish with some highly unusual flavours.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a smoke filled kitchen as the three chefs prepare their summer themed mains in the hope of impressing the veteran judge. Selin Kiazim is in her element, cooking a feast inspired by the summer barbecues she enjoyed with her family.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: All three chefs are feeling the pressure as they attempt their technical summer desserts. With only the two highest scorers going through to tomorrows regional final, who will be sent home?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges: nd for the first time this year, broadcaster and restaurateur Andi Oliver.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs have been challenged to create outstanding dishes that capture a `taste of summer. Their menus must reflect the tastes, smells and colours of everybodys favourite time of year.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and with a seafood specialist in the kitchen the competition is stepping up a gear. Tom is hoping hell be able to elevate the humble mackerel to the standards required for the Wimbledon banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With a refined summer barbecue going up against a traditional Cornish speciality and a historic Wimbledon related recipe, who will come out on top? Andy Clatworthy is using his passion for historical cooking to reinvent a dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: All three chefs are honouring Wimbledon and the British summer with their technically challenging strawberry desserts. Only the two highest scorers will go through to cook for the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs compete for a place in the national finals. To get there theyll have to impress the formidable panel of judges: Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and for the first time this year, broadcaster and restaurateur Andi Oliver.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tom Parker is eager to impress with a salad that includes 10 elements. Meanwhile, Paul Askew, hopes his traditional techniques will triumph over his younger competitors, and Ellis Barrie is taking a risk with a creative cucumber and oyster starter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen gets even tenser as the three newcomers begin to understand the perfection and skill the veteran chef demands. Ellis Barrie attempts to create the flavour of a summer barbecue.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course and the pressure is intense in the kitchen. In the hope of impressing the veteran judge, Paul Askew is serving an extremely personal main course based on his mothers favourite food.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Paul cant afford to make any mistakes and is pushing himself with a technically challenging baked alaska.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. The guest judge is Greg Rusedski, former British number one, who understands the high standards expected at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning banquet champion Tommy Banks takes on two ambitious newcomers Danny Parker and Josh Overington. They start by finding out whois the surprise veteran judge and who will be marking their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated, the three chefs are cooking their fish courses. Josh Overington, creates a decadent dish inspired by the prestige of the Wimbledon Championships.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are cooking their main courses and are all taking risks to try and win a place at the banquet. Danny Parker, is trying to recreate the flavours of an American barbecue but the veteran chef is dubious about one of his ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. All the chefs are using unusual ingredients in their desserts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the North East must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver, and guest Judy Murray.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Scottish regional heats and returning chefs to the competition Michael Bremner and Ally McGrath battle ambitious newcomer Angela Malik for a place in the national finals. This time they are cooking their starters.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated and the veteran chef is expecting perfection from the fish course. Ally McGrath creates an inventive dish based on his summer memories of rock pooling in the River Tweed.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Angela Malik is in her element, cooking a tiffin box based on memories of summer picnics. Michael Bremner is taking a huge risk, and Ally is creating an inventive dish based on a summer barbeque.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Angela Malik is cooking a technically challenging dessert.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two Scottish chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver. They are joined by guest judge Gary Parsons.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Determined newcomers Paul Croasdale and Nick Brodie take on returning chef Phil Carmichael for a place in the national finals. Its Phils third time in the competition and this year hes determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The pressure in the kitchen is high as the chefs prepare their fish courses. Paul Croasdale is trying to recreate the elegance of Wimbledon with luxury ingredients such as king crab and scallops.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and all three chefs are cooking Welsh lamb in hope of impressing. Phil is taking a risk with barbecued lamb and in an attempt to elevate a humble barbecue to banquet level, hes serving it with many accompaniments.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges so all three chefs are feeling the pressure. Phil Carmichael creates a tennis ball dessert that combines two British classics, Eton mess and trifle.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two winning chefs from the Wales region must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges and guest judge Dan Bloxham, Wimbledons Master of Ceremonies.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With two spots left in the National Finals, its the turn of the Central chefs to battle it out. Pip Lacey, head chef at the Michelin starred Murano in London, tried her luck in the competition two years ago. Shes takes on two newcomers.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With the chefs hoping to impress, they each take risks. Pip Lacey combines lobster with strawberry in a tribute to strawberries and cream. Ryan Simpsons also using lobster in his recreation of a BBQ and has invented a technique to create his chips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today the chefs cook their main courses and have very different takes on the Taste of Summer brief. Ryan Simpson hopes to elevate a ploughmans lunch into a dish worthy of the banquet, and Nick Deverell-Smith celebrates a meat he thinks is underused.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges. Pip Laceys pineapple dessert uses coriander. Nick Deverell-Smith also pays tribute to the pineapple, creating a dessert featuring a rice pudding made with cous-cous.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs whove made it through cook their four courses again. They must impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest judge Jordanne Whiley, one of Britains most successful tennis players.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs meet their surprise veteran judge and cook their starters. Eddie is hoping to do better than last year, Joery is hoping to impress with a tribute to Richard Krajicek, and Tommy is creating a warm salad.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Joery Castel is looking to triumph by combing mackerel and cucumber. Tommy Heaney hopes his Murray Mound is a fitting tribute to Andi Murray, Eddie Attwell is creating five different strawberry elements to accompany the scallops in his dish.

programme.desc: Tommy Heaneys BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castels aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwells taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles

programme.desc: Setting off from Liverpool, Tony takes a three-day walk inland to Wigan, tracing the story of the Leeds and Liverpool Canals chaotic construction and its dramatic impact on the development of Lancashire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat over two floors in Londons Camberwell, another flat in Telford in Shropshire and a three-bed semi in Alvaston in Derby are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a grand five-bedroom period house in Folkestone in Kent, a two bedroom flat in Londons Leytonstone and a two bedroom mid terrace in Fenton in Stoke on Trent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A studio flat in a former 1960s office block which is grade two listed in Londons Elephant and Castle, a terraced house in Pontygwaith in South Wales and a flat in in Cradley Heath near Birmingham are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with no floor in Edgbaston in Birmingham, a detached house on several levels in Oldham and a former garage complex ripe for development in Forest Hill in London are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A converted 16th century chapel in Matlock in Derbyshire, a two bedroom flat in Whetstone in London and a water damaged house in Wishaw in Scotland are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two bed semi in Meir in Stoke on Trent is given a country house look, a flat in Londons Willesden Green also gets the wow factor and a semi in Dudley in the West Midlands loses its 1980s look to be brought into the 21st century.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Terraced houses in Gravesend in Kent and Shipley in Derbyshire are given a new lease of life while former church premises in Harrow Weald in Middlesex are given a new use for the community.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion meet property developers who have all bought at auction and hope to make a profit on their investments. Today a landscape gardener tackles a terrace in Ilkeston in Derby, two friends take on a four storey house in Sittingbourne in Kent while another couple of friends first venture together is a studio flat in South Norwood in London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a stone cottage in Tilton on the Hill in rural Leicestershire while Martin Roberts is at a property auction buy in Croydon on the outskirts of London and Lucy Alexander meets two friends who have bought a bungalow in Rainham in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Chaddesdon in Derby, a two-bedroom flat in Londons Maida Vale and a property in Warrington are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a pretty cottage with outbuildings with huge potential in Burton upon Trent, while Lucy checks out a flat in Londons Crystal Palace and Dion is in Mossley near Manchester in a four-bedroom bungalow.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin tackles the issue of solar panels on a property in Mountain Ash in Wales and wonders if itll be a sunny outcome for the new owner, while Lucy looks round a barn in Elstree in Hertfordshire and Dion visits a flat in Newport in Shropshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets a couple who buy a former millworkers cottage in Derbyshire as their first home together; Lucy Alexander meets a father and daughter team who are seasoned developers buying a house in Kent; and Dion Dublin talks to a man who, along with two friends, has clubbed together to buy an investment property in Lowestoft in Suffolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Walsall in the Midlands looking around a 1950s semi; Martin Roberts meets a man developing a ground-floor one-bedroom flat in Ilford in Essex; and Lucy Alexander catches up on a multi-million-pound redevelopment in East Horsley in Surrey where one small cottage with a big garden is converted into eight separate properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with a bathroom layout challenge in Ripley, Derbyshire, a huge three-storey house with its freehold on offer in Erdington, Birmingham, and a mid-terrace house in Londons East Ham in need of a major makeover are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Lincolnshire looking round a pair of rural semi-detached cottages which the new owner may convert into one home. Martin Roberts is in Cheshire at a bungalow with bags of potential, and Lucy Alexander is in Kent talking to friends embarking on their first project together.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house in Chesterton in Staffordshire has a smelly problem and needs a total overhaul. The new owner of a semi in Bestwood in Nottingham also has a lot to do, while the new owner of a flat in Strawberry Hill has less than a week to sort out her auction buy before she jets off to Germany.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A husband and wife duo add their design skills to a house in Croydon in Surrey, while a seasoned developer creates a high-end house in multiple occupation in Coventry and a first time developer also does a great job with a house full of period features in Nutall in Nottinghamshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets one of two young brothers busy building a property portfolio in Bolsover, Derbyshire, while Dion Dublin checks out a three-bedroom semi in Wolverhampton, and Lucy Alexander is impressed by plans to convert a two-bedroom, fire-damaged terraced house in Londons Plaistow into a smart five-bedroom property.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin has a hair-raising experience at a barbers in Rochdale, Lancashire, while Martin Roberts looks round a great bungalow in Alsager, Cheshire, and Lucy Alexander sees all the potential in a piece of land in Eccles in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.desc: Miller comes under suspicion during an investigation, Walker gets the results of his sergeants exams, and David decides that he wants to set up his own playboy mansion.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker announces his plans to leave the village - but will he really be able to say goodbye to Aidensfield for good? Meanwhile, an Irish woman becomes the target of local hostility.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The peace of idyllic Badgers Drift is shattered by an inexplicable murder. It falls to DCI Barnaby and Sergeant Troy to hunt down the killer. Their investigation uncovers a web of sinister events, some long buried in the past.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When a gang of bikers cause havoc in Aidensfield, the local residents declare that enough is enough. Meanwhile, new PC Joe Mason arrives on the scene, but will his tactics result in a warm welcome or the cold shoulder from his new colleagues and the locals?

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sergeant Miller is on the warpath - especially when PC Mason arrests Lord Ashfordly, and David makes a will, but soon gets paranoid that somebody is out to get him.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An accident during the restoration of a canal leads to a particularly difficult case for Barnaby and Troy, which appears to be linked to a group of rowdy teenagers.

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Whispers that Cowley General contains a cursed ward - and a seemingly innocuous death - set off a chain of events that lead Endeavour to investigate.

programme.title: Trial and Retribution
programme.desc: When well-known paediatric surgeon Jonathan Carlisle is found dead, DCI Connor uncovers conflicts stirred by a recent operation that resulted in a young girls death. The team soon learn that the girls father, Gary Webster, was the subject of a restraining order issued by Carlisle, and had been stalking him for months. However, before he can be questioned, Webster absconds.

programme.title: Trial and Retribution
programme.desc: Connor instructs her deflated team to stay on top of the investigation as details of Carlisles private life start to emerge. Walker and Connor return to the hospital to investigate the possibility of a negligence cover-up, but what was the real motive for Amber Websters brutal murder?

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants

programme.sub-title: Of Mice and Munks; Simon Saves the World

programme.desc: Rosemary Pauline West is an English serial killer who collaborated with her husband, Fred West, in the torture and murder of at least nine young women between 1973 and 1987. She also murdered her eight-year-old stepdaughter, Charmaine, in 1971. Delve into the psychology of Rose to try and understand what turned her into such a cruel rapist and murderer.

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld

programme.title: Conviction: Murder at the Station
programme.desc: Following a team of experts as they investigate whether evidence exists that could challenge the conviction of a man in prison for a murder he denies. Every year, over 500 people in the UK are convicted of murder. A guilty verdict can only be challenged if new evidence comes to light. Inside Justice is a specialist charity led by Louise Shorter, supported by a panel of criminal lawyers, ex-police officers and forensics experts who offer assistance to prisoners who are protesting their innocence.

programme.title: Conviction: Murder at the Station
programme.desc: Louise investigates a case, looking for evidence that could present grounds to bring an appeal, and ultimately overturn the conviction of a Southampton man in prison for the murder of his secret lover.

programme.title: When Fred Met Rose
programme.desc: Documentary series examining what led Fred and Rose West to become serial killers.

programme.title: Fred and Rose: The West Murders
programme.desc: Focuses on the notorious house which the couple shared in Gloucester. Fred and Rose murdered their first three victims - Anna McFall, Charmaine West and then Rena Costello - because they `got in the way. But when they moved into 25 Cromwell Street, the couple began to kill for sexual thrills.

programme.title: Fred and Rose: The West Murders

programme.desc: When pretty but selfish Mary Ashburn makes a dramatic exit from her husbands life, the distraught doctor cannot imagine ever being happy and loved again.

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A retired man plans to run away with his mistress and stolen company funds but his wife has something else in mind.

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A man and his lover (Mark Miller, Patricia Breslin) plot to eliminate his wife (Vivienne Segal) so they can be together.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the highly-innovative filmmaker known for `Titanic, `Avatar and `The Terminator.

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A fascinating look at the life and career of Shirley MacLaine, one of Hollywoods legendary leading ladies who starred in films including The Apartment and Sweet Charity.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Being admired by the inventor of an anti-ageing formula is handy for Caroline, who is eager to keep her looks, but a handsome man forces her to make a big decision.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Criminal lawyer Slade has his reasons for wanting his colleague dead. He meticulously plans the perfect murder, but theres one thing that he overlooks.

programme.title: Painting Birds with Jim and Nancy Moir
programme.desc: Jim and Nancy Moir head to Northern Ireland to catch a glimpse of the colourful puffin, taking in the landscapes and creating some unique paintings featuring these remarkable seabirds.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Pitching easels and priming canvases to find out who is the Landscape Artist of the Year. Hopeful contestants try to paint their way to a life-changing cash prize in various locations across the country.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of an Oscar-winning director renowned for his experimental approach and hits such as Erin Brockovich and Oceans Eleven.

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: Film experts reflect on the work of Charles Bronson, who became a Hollywood star with his tough-guy, vigilante roles in films such as The Magnificent Seven and Death Wish.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Vietnam veteran Fred Pearson is a broken man after years of punishment from his sergeant. But years later, he gets his chance for revenge.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.sub-title: The Best Chess Player in the World
programme.desc: To prove he is a gifted chess player, a man explains how he planned the perfect murder after discovering his wife was unfaithful.

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: An undertaker (John Fielder) attempts to collect from a penny-pinching killer (Ed Gardner).

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A seedy character (Frankie Darro) discovers an amazing formula and decides to use it to make some quick cash. With Robert Keith and Karl Lukas.

programme.title: Andre Rieu: I Lost My Heart in Heidelberg
programme.desc: The violin sensation leads a spectacular musical tour of the German town, giving sensational performances from a romantic ruined castle and a boat on the river Neckar.

programme.title: Pavarotti - A Voice for the Ages
programme.desc: Half a million people flocked to hear the maestro in Central Park and millions worldwide watched on television. Audiences adored his larger than life personality, childlike charm, generous figure, and happy go lucky style. To his adoring fans, the tenor became a titan of popular culture.

programme.title: Hugh Grant: Love Language
programme.desc: Explore the charming and complex career of British actor Hugh Grant, whose wit, charisma, and vulnerability made him a defining figure in romantic comedies. Delve into Grants ability to capture the nuances of love, relationships, and connection.

programme.title: Sparkplug
programme.desc: Manchester, 1983. Dave loves Rod Stewart, his girlfriend and his Ford Capri. Hes all set for a new start. Only his girlfriend is about to have someone elses baby. He struggles with whether he is ready to be a Dad.

programme.title: Sparkplug
programme.desc: Manchester, 1983. Dave loves Rod Stewart, his girlfriend and his Ford Capri. Hes all set for a new start. Only his girlfriend is about to have someone elses baby. He struggles with whether he is ready to be a Dad.

programme.title: The Cinema List: Bingeable
programme.desc: On the Cinema List, join us as we explore our favourite franchises to get cosy with, from Transformers to Bad Boys 2. Snuggle down on the sofa and take your pick.

programme.title: A Fistful of Dollars
programme.desc: Sergio Leones classic about a mysterious drifters involvement with two families, the Baxters and the Rojos, in a Mexican border town. Instead of leaving town, the drifter gets caught up in the feud and plays both sides of the warring factions and gets rich in the bargain.

programme.title: The Patriot
programme.desc: A widowed farmer, with a brave but brutal military past, decides not to join up when the British arrive in 1776. However, when his son enlists and is later captured by the enemy, the former soldier must abandon his new-found pacifist principles in order to rescue his oldest child, and forms a regiment of Carolina patriots, whose guerrilla tactics prove pivotal to the US war effort.

programme.title: The Legend of Tarzan
programme.desc: Lured from Victorian England to the wilds of the Congo, Tarzan must return to the jungle to save his captive wife from King Leopolds devious envoy.

programme.title: Kong: Skull Island
programme.desc: Scientists, soldiers and adventurers encounter the mighty Kong and other monstrous creatures after travelling to an uncharted island in the Pacific Ocean.

programme.title: Transformers: The Last Knight
programme.desc: As war with the Transformers rages on, inventor Cade Yeager forms an unlikely alliance with Bumblebee, an English lord and an Oxford professor to save the planet.

programme.title: Spider-Man 2
programme.desc: The comic book web-slinger returns for a blockbusting sequel. Crime-fighting has cost Peter Parker his best friend and his true love Mary Jane. However, just as he quits as Spider-Man, New York comes under threat from Dr Octopus, a metal-tentacled madman. Peter has no choice but to swing back into action.

programme.title: Birds of Prey (and the Fantabulous Emancipation of One Harley Quinn)
programme.desc: After splitting with the Joker, Harley Quinn joins superheroes Black Canary, Huntress and Renee Montoya to save a young girl from an evil crime lord, Black Mask in Gotham City.

programme.title: Fast and Furious Presents: Hobbs and Shaw
programme.desc: Brixton Lorr is a cybernetically enhanced soldier who possesses superhuman strength, a brilliant mind and a lethal pathogen that could wipe out half of the worlds population. Its now up to hulking lawman Luke Hobbs and lawless operative Deckard Shaw to put aside their past differences and work together to prevent the seemingly indestructible Lorr from destroying humanity.

programme.title: The Fall Guy
programme.desc: Battle-scarred stuntman Colt Seavers springs back into action when a movie star suddenly disappears. As the mystery deepens, he soon finds himself ensnared in a sinister plot that pushes him to the edge of a fall more dangerous than any stunt.

programme.title: Black Hawk Down
programme.desc: Based on the botched mission during Americas military involvement in Somalia in the early 1990s. An attempt by US Army Rangers parachuted into Mogadishu to capture a warlords two top lieutenants goes disastrously wrong when two helicopters are shot down.

programme.title: Black Hawk Down
programme.desc: Based on the botched mission during Americas military involvement in Somalia in the early 1990s. An attempt by US Army Rangers parachuted into Mogadishu to capture a warlords two top lieutenants goes disastrously wrong when two helicopters are shot down.

programme.title: Rebellious
programme.desc: A headstrong princess is stolen by an evil sorcerer, and her scholarly fiance must use his wits in an epic quest to find her while she boldly challenges her captors and plots a daring escape.

programme.title: Batman Unlimited: Animal Instincts
programme.desc: When the beast-themed Animilitia plague Gotham, Batman swings out to investigate. But with The Penguin and his cyber-animals added to the mix, Batman will need the help of Green Arrow and The Flash to bring them to heel.

programme.title: Fox and Hare Save the Forest
programme.desc: One day, Fox and Hare discover that Owl has disappeared. They go looking for him in the forest with all their friends and discover a big lake that wasnt there before. It is the beginning of an exciting and funny adventure.

programme.title: Rock Dog
programme.desc: Hoping to become a famous musician, a young Tibetan Mastiff leaves home to find a legendary and reclusive music artist, setting off a series of unexpected events.

programme.title: The LEGO Movie 2: The Second Part
programme.desc: The citizens of Bricksburg face a dangerous new threat when LEGO DUPLO invaders from outer space start to wreck everything in their path. The battle to defeat the enemy and restore harmony to the LEGO universe takes Emmet, Lucy, Batman and the rest of their friends to faraway, unexplored worlds that test their courage and creativity.

programme.title: Rango
programme.desc: An ordinary chameleon, Rango, winds up in the town of Dirt, a lawless outpost in the Wild West in need of a new sheriff.

programme.title: Despicable Me 4
programme.desc: Gru welcomes a new member to the family, Gru Jr., whos intent on tormenting his dad. However, their peaceful existence soon comes crashing down when criminal mastermind Maxime Le Mal escapes from prison and vows revenge against Gru.

programme.title: Flushed Away
programme.desc: A spoiled rat gets flushed down the toilet and finds himself stranded in Londons sewer underworld. A savvy scavenger helps him evade frog assassins and make his way back to his plush home.

programme.title: The Angry Birds Movie
programme.desc: Flightless birds lead a mostly happy existence, except for Red, who just cant get past the daily annoyances of life. His temperament leads him to anger management class, where he meets fellow misfits Chuck and Bomb. Red becomes even more agitated when his feathered brethren welcome green pigs to their island paradise. As the swine begin to get under his skin, Red joins forces with Chuck and Bomb to investigate the real reason behind their mysterious arrival.

programme.title: The Angry Birds Movie 2
programme.desc: Red, Chuck, Bomb and the rest of their feathered friends are surprised when a green pig suggests that they put aside their differences and unite to fight a common threat. Aggressive birds from an island covered in ice are planning to use an elaborate weapon to destroy the fowl and swine way of life. After picking their best and brightest, the birds and pigs come up with a scheme to infiltrate the island, deactivate the device and return to their respective paradises intact.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Dracula, who operates a high-end resort away from the human world, goes into overprotective mode when a boy discovers the resort and falls for the counts teen-aged daughter.

programme.title: Turbo
programme.desc: A snail gets the chance to escape his slow-paced life and race in the Indianapolis 500 when a freak accident gives him the power of superspeed.

programme.title: Batman Unlimited: Animal Instincts
programme.desc: When the beast-themed Animilitia plague Gotham, Batman swings out to investigate. But with The Penguin and his cyber-animals added to the mix, Batman will need the help of Green Arrow and The Flash to bring them to heel.

programme.title: Rock Dog
